---
title: "UIAO Modernization Master Plan — Glossary"
subtitle: "Acronyms and key concepts used across the master plan series"
doc-type: glossary
audience: "All master-plan readers"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
date: 2026-04-26
---

# UIAO Modernization Master Plan — Glossary {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## Series companion
This glossary is shared by all five master-plan documents:
[Series index](index.qmd) ·
[Phase 0 Planning](phase-0-planning.qmd) ·
[Phase 1 Architecture](phase-1-architecture.qmd) ·
[Boundary Modernization Appendix](boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd) ·
[Compliance & ATO Alignment](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd) ·
[90-Day Execution Plan](90-day-execution-plan.qmd)
:::

---

## Acronyms

| Acronym | Expansion |
|---|---|
| **3PAO** | Third-Party Assessment Organization |
| **AD** | Active Directory |
| **ADR** | Architecture Decision Record |
| **AO** | Authorizing Official |
| **ATO** | Authority to Operate |
| **AU** | Administrative Unit (Microsoft Entra ID) |
| **cATO** | Continuous Authority to Operate |
| **CIO** | Chief Information Officer |
| **CISA** | Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency |
| **CISO** | Chief Information Security Officer |
| **EDR** | Endpoint Detection and Response |
| **EO** | Executive Order |
| **FedRAMP** | Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program |
| **GCC** | Government Community Cloud |
| **HR** | Human Resources |
| **IdP** | Identity Provider |
| **ISSO** | Information System Security Officer |
| **KSI** | Key Security Indicator |
| **LDAP** | Lightweight Directory Access Protocol |
| **M365** | Microsoft 365 |
| **MFA** | Multi-Factor Authentication |
| **NIST** | National Institute of Standards and Technology |
| **OMB** | Office of Management and Budget |
| **OSCAL** | Open Security Controls Assessment Language |
| **OU** | Organizational Unit |
| **PIM** | Privileged Identity Management |
| **PMO** | Program Management Office |
| **POA&M** | Plan of Action and Milestones |
| **RACI** | Responsible / Accountable / Consulted / Informed |
| **Rev 5** | Revision 5 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5; FedRAMP Rev 5 baselines) |
| **SAR** | Security Assessment Report |
| **SASE** | Secure Access Service Edge |
| **SCuBA** | Secure Cloud Business Applications (CISA) |
| **SD-WAN** | Software-Defined Wide Area Network |
| **SIEM** | Security Information and Event Management |
| **SP** | Special Publication (e.g., NIST SP 800-207) |
| **SRF** | Signal Restoration Framework (Boundary Appendix §4) |
| **SSP** | System Security Plan |
| **UIAO** | Unified Identity-Addressing-Overlay (the architecture this master plan governs) |
| **WAN** | Wide Area Network |

---

## Mandates and Frameworks

| Reference | Description |
|---|---|
| **EO 14028** | Executive Order on Improving the Nation's Cybersecurity (2021) |
| **OMB M-22-09** | Federal Zero Trust Architecture strategy memorandum |
| **OMB M-24-15** | Continuous monitoring and ongoing authorization expectations memorandum |
| **NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5** | Security and privacy controls catalog |
| **NIST SP 800-207** | Zero Trust Architecture reference model |
| **FedRAMP Rev 5** | Cloud-service authorization baseline (Low / Moderate / High) |
| **CISA SCuBA** | Secure configuration baselines for M365 and cloud business applications |
| **NIST OSCAL** | Machine-readable representation of catalogs, profiles, components, SSP, SAR, POA&M |

---

## Key Concepts

| Term | Definition |
|---|---|
| **Architectural Plane** | A first-class architectural unit with a defined responsibility, signal surface, interface contract, and control bindings. UIAO has ten planes (Phase 1 §2.1). |
| **Arbitration (SRF L5)** | Resolution of conflicting signals from multiple sources via canonical authority; recorded as a Compliance-plane assertion (Boundary §4.2). |
| **Attributed Degradation** | A governed exception in which a signal class is not preserved at a boundary crossing; requires named compensating control, re-acquisition mechanism, and drift threshold (Boundary §3.2). |
| **Authorization Boundary** | The surface across which signals, identities, and traffic transition between domains of differing trust, control, or compliance authority. |
| **Authorization-Conforming** | A deployment that satisfies the full conformance criterion set across architecture, boundary, and compliance dimensions (Compliance §7). |
| **Authorization-State** | The single derived value, produced by the Continuous ATO plane, representing current ongoing-authorization posture (Compliance §5.1). |
| **Boundary Class** | One of six categories of authorization-boundary crossing: Identity, Network, Tenant, Plane, Telemetry, Authorization (Boundary §2). |
| **Boundary Crossing** | A discrete event in which traffic, identity, or signal traverses a boundary; produces a `boundary-crossing` provenance record. |
| **Boundary-Conforming** | A boundary redesign that satisfies all eight Boundary Appendix §6 conformance criteria, including sustained posture. |
| **Boundary-Evidenced** | A boundary redesign that satisfies §6 criteria 1–4 (inventoried, captured, continuity-matrix-satisfied, attributed-degradation-governed) but not yet criteria 5–8. |
| **Boundary Gap** | The structural condition in which the current authorization boundary does not preserve a coherent set of identity, location, session, telemetry, media, and policy signals end-to-end (Phase 0 §4). |
| **Canon-Change Protocol** | The governed process under which canonical artifacts (architecture, schemas, controls) are revised. Substantive changes require an ADR, schema check, and drift scan. |
| **Capture (SRF L4)** | Unconditional emission of a provenance-record at every boundary crossing, including crossings that preserve all signals (Boundary §4.2 invariant 1). |
| **Compensating Control** | A control accepted by the AO as a substitute for full signal preservation at a boundary crossing where attributed degradation applies (Boundary §3.2). |
| **Conformance Criterion** | A demonstrable, evidence-backed condition a deployment must satisfy to be considered architecturally, boundary-, evidence-, or authorization-conforming. |
| **Continuity Matrix** | The normative table specifying, for each boundary class × signal class pair, whether end-to-end preservation is required (R) or attributed degradation is permitted (A) (Boundary §3.1). |
| **Continuous ATO (cATO)** | Operating posture under which authorization is sustained continuously rather than periodically renewed, derived from canonical evidence. |
| **Control Binding** | Typed attachment of a control to one or more planes, with cited signal class, evidence pattern, cadence, and arbitration rule (Phase 1 §5; Compliance §2). |
| **Deterministic State** | The first architectural principle: every governed entity has exactly one canonical state at any point in time. |
| **Drift** | Classified divergence between expected and observed state. Five categories: Schema, Value, Hierarchy, Orphan, Phantom. |
| **Evidence Chain** | The append-only, attributed, schema-conformant graph of provenance-records from which all compliance assertions derive (Compliance §4). |
| **Evidence-Conforming** | A deployment satisfying Compliance §7 criteria 1–4 (controls bound or POA&M-tracked, bindings meet quality bar, KSIs derived per rule, evidence chain has six properties). |
| **KSI (Key Security Indicator)** | A compact, AO-facing expression of compliance posture, derived from canonical control state per Compliance §3.1. Three tiers (AO, ISSO, operator). |
| **OrgPath** | The canonical attribute set that encodes a principal's hierarchical and delegation context, replacing implicit OU-tree governance. |
| **Plane Layering** | The four-tier rule (Substrate / Enforcement / Assurance / Authorization) governing which plane may consume which signal (Phase 1 §2.2). |
| **POA&M Closure Rule** | Closure requires that the triggering gap is no longer present in the chain, the closure event is itself a provenance-record, and closure does not depend on attributed degradation exceeding threshold (Compliance §6.2). |
| **Provenance Record** | An immutable, append-only, schema-conformant record of any state change or enforcement decision; the atomic unit of the evidence chain. |
| **Reconstruct (SRF L2)** | Rebuild signal context lost in transit, citing canonical sources only — never heuristic synthesis (Boundary §4.2 invariant 2). |
| **Re-attest (SRF L3)** | Re-establish posture or authentication where carry-through is not technically feasible. |
| **Signal Class** | One of eight canonical signal categories: six primary (Identity, Location, Session, Telemetry, Media, Policy) plus two derived (Provenance, Drift) (Phase 1 §4). |
| **Signal Continuity** | The end-to-end preservation of a signal class across all boundary crossings; the architectural goal of the Boundary Modernization plane. |
| **Signal Restoration Framework (SRF)** | The five-layer normative pattern (Carry, Reconstruct, Re-attest, Capture, Arbitrate) by which signals are preserved across boundary crossings (Boundary §4). |
| **Substrate-Conforming** | A deployment satisfying Phase 1 §7 criteria 1–4 (planes present, interfaces fixed, layering enforced, signals carriable) but not yet authorization-conforming. |
| **Two-Brain Execution** | The architectural principle separating governance logic (Copilot) from execution logic (PowerShell, Graph API, etc.); they may not co-mingle. |
| **UIAO** | Unified Identity-Addressing-Overlay — the canonical substrate this master plan governs. |
| **Zero Trust** | The architectural posture in which access decisions bind to canonical identity, device posture, and session risk at every enforcement point (NIST SP 800-207, OMB M-22-09). |

---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
## Document Governance
This glossary is a derived consumer of the master plan series. Definitions
trace to the upstream document and section cited; substantive divergence
between this glossary and an upstream document is glossary drift, not an
upstream change. Updates to the upstream definitions are reflected here
on the same canon-change cycle.
:::
